failure_hints.text: Coherence as quality signal treats the internal consistency and narrative flow of an argument as evidence of its correctness — well-constructed wrong analyses feel more true than messy correct ones.
Confidence of ignorance makes less-informed decision-makers more confident than better-informed ones, because fewer contradictions and gaps allow a more coherent story to form.
A team can keep using an inherited validation habit after the real failure surface has moved somewhere else.
Analogies can be misapplied when the team borrows a reference class without checking whether the current case actually fits the underlying assumptions.
Cognitive clutter can defeat the point of the checklist when the list becomes too verbose or irrelevant to guide action.
Checklist completion can create false confidence when the list never tested whether the underlying assumptions were still true.

Escalation of commitment generates cumulative lock-in through a chain of small rationalizations — each dissonance-resolution step makes the next sunk-cost investment easier to justify and the next contrary signal easier to dismiss.
Evidence avoidance deprioritizes the feedback mechanisms, review meetings, and external assessments that would generate the dissonance-intensifying contrary signals.
Reflection can become a hiding place that postpones commitment, trade-offs, or the next concrete move.
Perfectionism can turn the pause into analysis paralysis rather than a better-sequenced action path.

heuristic_hints.text: Apply outside-view reference-class data to import evidence not in the current picture — base rates provide exactly the missing context that System 1's narrative would otherwise exclude.
Before committing to any confident conclusion, explicitly audit what information is missing and whether the absence is random or structured by selection or incentive.
Use a small trusted set of queries, checks, or benchmarks to score whether the method behaves the way the team expects.
Check a new case against the full assumption set of the reference class before trusting the analogy.
Enforce completeness by using a checklist to cover all distinct branches of the problem without overlap.
Keep the checklist simple and concise so it reduces load instead of adding noise.

Celebrate public updating: when leaders visibly change their position based on evidence, they demonstrate that intellectual updating is rewarded rather than penalized — directly countering the identity-threat that powers dissonance.
Establish explicit kill criteria before committing resources: pre-define the conditions that would trigger termination, so that the exit decision is made by the earlier, less-dissonant self.
Reduce the problem into logical pieces only after the needed output is clear enough that the extra analysis will serve a real decision.
Switch from intuitive reaction to deliberate framing before committing to a plan in high-stakes or ambiguous situations.

premortem_hints.text: If the person preparing this briefing had wanted to show us a different conclusion, what would they have included that is not here?
Is this conclusion well-supported, or is it well-constructed?
Is this evidence time-tested because it is truly predictive, or just because it is familiar?
What would have to be true for this reference class or validation test to actually fit this case?
Does this checklist accurately capture the essential complexity of this specific problem, or am I trying to fit a square peg into a round hole?
Is this process driving commitment and action, or merely enforcing a bureaucratic tick-box mentality?
Are we staying this course because the evidence supports it, or because changing course would constitute a public admission of error?
If this same project were brought fresh today with the data we now have, would we approve it — or are we continuing because stopping would feel like admitting an error?
Is it the most important thing you could be doing right now?
What is the core point, and why does it matter?

select_when: A confident conclusion has been reached from evidence that may be incomplete, non-random, or selectively presented.
One party controls the information available to the other and the recipient has no signal of what is absent.
A decision is being made from a briefing, pitch, or recommendation that tells a coherent story — and the coherence itself feels like a quality signal.
Time pressure or urgency is preventing the 'what is missing?' question from being asked before commitment.
Visible winners, successful cases, or confirming instances have been assembled without an equal accounting of failures or absences.
danger_when: Evidence has been explicitly audited for completeness and the gaps are known and acknowledged — the closure is warranted.
The model is being used to introduce unlimited epistemic paralysis rather than structured gap identification.

select_when: A claim, idea, or method looks promising but needs to be checked against proven tests, reference classes, or established validation loops before wider commitment.
The team is at risk of trusting novelty, analogy, or polished explanation without enough evidence that the approach actually works.
Repeated validation against known standards, expected answers, or old-but-reliable checks would reduce the chance of fashionable error.
A recommendation needs credibility that comes from surviving structured verification, not just from sounding coherent.
danger_when: Time-tested validation is being used to block necessary novelty even though the situation is genuinely new and existing reference classes fit poorly.
The team is treating inherited validation habits as sacred without checking whether they still test the real failure surface.

select_when: Omission risk is higher than invention risk, especially where failure comes from skipped steps, forgotten prerequisites, or weak handoffs.
The process has known failure points, approvals, or dependency checks that must be verified every time.
A recommendation needs minimum viable operating discipline before action, during execution, and before scaling.
Working memory limits are turning a complex multi-step task into cognitive clutter or inconsistent execution.
danger_when: The checklist is being treated as a substitute for checking whether the assumptions, environment, or causal story are still true.
The situation is novel or emergent enough that a generic checklist would force reality into a familiar but mismatched frame.

select_when: A public, ego-identified commitment has been made and subsequent evidence challenges it — the risk is motivated belief revision rather than honest updating.
A team is deep into a failing initiative and contrary evidence is being reframed, minimized, or explained away rather than genuinely weighted.
A decision-maker's stated reasoning is suspiciously well-aligned with the conclusion that protects their prior commitment.
Evidence avoidance is occurring — feedback mechanisms, review channels, or external assessments are being deprioritized without explicit justification.
Post-hoc rationalization is being offered as the original reasoning — the justification was constructed after the commitment, not before.
danger_when: Genuine honest reconsideration has occurred and the position update is evidence-driven rather than dissonance-motivated — the model should not pathologize real belief updating.
The model is being used to dismiss any position-maintenance as rationalization without examining whether the evidence genuinely warrants the maintained position.
identity_chunks.output_type: motivated belief revision detection and escalation warning

select_when: Immediate immersion is obscuring the structure of the problem and a deliberate pause would likely improve the next move.

A high-stakes decision needs the thinker to shift from intuitive reaction toward more deliberate analysis.
The team needs to recheck alignment with the bigger picture before more effort is spent on a local branch.
danger_when: The pause is becoming a hiding place for avoiding commitment, trade-offs, or concrete next steps.
The team is already clear on the problem and now needs execution rather than another round of reflective abstraction.

reframings.reframed_question: What evidence from my network conversations would falsify the assumption that they represent a viable pipeline for launch?
reframings.what_opens: Path to test conversion rates by seeking specific commitments or LOIs from the 4-5 contacts, revealing true pipeline strength before burning runway.

reframings.reframed_question: What if I delayed launch until securing at least one signed LOI from my 4-5 network contacts, even if it extends beyond 6 weeks?
reframings.what_opens: Explores optionality of aligning timing with validated demand rather than fixed calendar, preserving 8-month runway.

dimensions.coverage_evidence: Assistant presents options: Option 1 delays 2-3 months to convert network to LOIs (explains employer notice risk); Option 2 launches now but accepts months 1-3 as BD (revenue pressure tradeoff); Option 3 adds fractional bridge; final rec: do 3 things in 6 weeks, push back only if no commitments after 4 weeks (sequencing rationale: respond to signal, not nervousness).

Assistant flags quitting forecloses job return/trajectory lock-in implicitly via delay options preserving current job; recommends staging commitment (check spouse/network in 4 weeks before final push); distinguishes sunk planning from future costs (nervousness not signal); Option 3 fractional as reversible bridge vs full launch.

Identifies spouse as key stakeholder (support in concept vs specifics); insists on specific runway/math convo this week (influence strategy: share burn rate, pressure scenarios); flags employer noticing BD as resistance risk in delay option; differentiates technical plan from organizational feasibility.

Mentions 8-month runway and burn rate but does not identify displaced alternatives (e.g., staying employed, other career uses of time/experience) or compare marginal returns of launch vs delay/bridge; no sizing of constraint binding across options.
dimensions.materiality_note: High materiality: Allocating full independence now vs partial (fractional) or delay forgoes current salary/other opportunities, could reverse launch rec if returns comparison favors delay.

dimensions.coverage_evidence: Names risks (zero pipeline conversion <1/5, 3-5 month first engagement, month 5 pressure into bad work) but does not size downside exposure (e.g., probability-bounded financial hit), distinguish mitigate (delay) vs adapt (fractional), or detail recovery paths (e.g., re-employment if fails).
dimensions.materiality_note: High materiality: Different risk posture (more mitigate via delay) could shift from viable launch to strong no-go if tails sized.

dimensions.coverage_evidence: Acknowledges network conversion uncertainty (<1/5 typical) but treats as estimable risk without classifying (epistemic: reducible via specific asks) vs unknowable; no matching strategy (e.g., experiments to reduce) or calibrated confidence.
dimensions.materiality_note: Medium materiality: Recognizing higher Knightian uncertainty in client behavior could favor more optionality (delay), altering staged launch rec.

dimensions.coverage_evidence: No discussion of network incentives (politeness vs real intent), monitoring commercial intent, or divergence (e.g., clients defecting post-launch); spouse alignment touches but not principal-agent frame.
dimensions.materiality_note: Immaterial: Core issue is demand validation, not agency problems; fractional touches but doesn't reverse rec.

dimensions.coverage_evidence: Assistant nudges user from status quo optimism ('treating conversations as pipeline because wanted to be') via specific asks (fractional $12K/month) and checkpoints (4 weeks); addresses persistence of wishful thinking; sustainability via staged commitment.

questions: What other personal or financial commitments, like family expenses or investments, are competing with the 8-month runway for your available resources?
If you reallocated some of your savings or time from the 6-week launch prep to something else, like extending the runway, what would that look like?
What abundance of resources, such as unused professional networks or skills from your 12 years in B2B fintech, are you not yet factoring into your launch plan?
What specific risks, beyond the zero-revenue runway, like health issues or market changes in fintech, worry you most about launching in 6 weeks?
How has your approach to risks changed compared to past career moves, like leaving previous roles?
What would adapting to the pipeline shortage look like for you versus trying to mitigate it entirely before launch?
Which parts of your 4-5 network conversations feel like measurable risks you can quantify, versus true unknowns about client commitments?
What ignorance gaps in the fintech consulting market, such as current demand for independent B2B specialists, are you operating under right now?
How much of the 8-month runway pressure is based on known industry timelines for first engagements, versus unpredictable factors like client decision cycles?
What incentives are your 4-5 network contacts operating under that might prevent their 'we'd consider you' comments from turning into actual engagements?
How would you structure monitoring or incentives with a fractional employer to ensure their needs align with your goal of building a full independent practice?
In what ways might your spouse's incentives around financial stability differ from yours on the specifics of the 5-months-of-revenue-pressure scenario?

triage_scores.evidence: User initially seeks quick tactical launch plan to hit the ground running in 6 weeks, but assistant explicitly challenges and refuses to close on premature tactics without addressing pipeline and runway realities.
User frames 6-week timeline around Q3 planning end, but assistant questions and recommends conditional delay based on signals rather than rushing under timeline pressure.
Assistant challenges user's overoptimistic pipeline conversion and runway assumptions head-on in Turn 2, rather than inheriting or enabling them.
Faint user admission of treating network as pipeline 'because I wanted them to be' (Turn 4), but assistant does not exhibit or amplify self-overappraisal.
Assistant flags user's denial in keeping runway specifics from spouse (Turn 5) and prompts confronting it, but conversation transaction surfaces rather than distorts the painful realities.

Assistant persistently probes user's assumptions (pipeline reality, runway math, spouse alignment, timing), introduces novel options like fractional arrangements, and encourages 4-week checkpoint with real data, driving superior decision process through exploratory questioning.

companion_fingerprint_raw.reasoning_move: Gather key clarifying information on pipeline, runway assumptions, and launch timing before providing tactical advice to ensure solving the right problem.
evidence_quotes: Before diving into tactics, can I ask a few things to make sure we're solving the right problem.

First: what's your current pipeline of consulting leads? Specifically, do you have prospective clients who've said "yes, once you're available, we want to engage you," or are you planning to build the pipeline during the transition?
Second: the 8 months runway — does that assume zero revenue, or is there an assumed ramp (like $0 for 2 months, then gradually increasing)? Those are different financial plans.
Third: the 6-week transition — why 6 weeks specifically? Is that notice period, last day aligned with something at your current job, or did you pick a number that felt right?
companion_fingerprint_raw.evidence_rationale: These quotes show the assistant prioritizing diagnostic questions over tactics to validate assumptions.

companion_fingerprint_raw.reasoning_move: Challenge optimistic pipeline assumptions by citing low conversion rates from informal interest to actual engagements.

companion_fingerprint_raw.evidence_rationale: Direct quote debunks the user's pipeline perception with probabilistic evidence.

companion_fingerprint_raw.reasoning_move: Assess runway adequacy against industry benchmarks for first-time consultants, highlighting risks of financial pressure.
evidence_quotes: 8 months at zero revenue is tight for a first-time independent consultant. Industry experience suggests the first paid engagement often takes 3-5 months from launch, not including the 1-2 months you'll spend setting up legal/admin.
companion_fingerprint_raw.evidence_rationale: Quote uses empirical timelines to quantify runway insufficiency.

companion_fingerprint_raw.reasoning_move: Prioritize addressing shaky fundamentals over tactical details, as tactics fail without solid foundations.
evidence_quotes: the tactical advice — pricing, positioning, website, legal structure — only matters if the fundamentals are solid. Right now the fundamentals are shaky in a specific way that a lot of first-time independents don't see until they're 4 months in and burning through savings.
companion_fingerprint_raw.evidence_rationale: Explicitly subordinates tactics to fundamentals with forward-looking risk illustration.

companion_fingerprint_raw.reasoning_move: Generate structured decision options with tradeoffs, incorporating risk tolerance and external stakeholder input.
evidence_quotes: Here are three real versions, with different tradeoffs. Option 1: Delay launch by 2-3 months... Option 2: Launch on your current timeline but accept that months 1-3 are business development... Option 3: Launch on current timeline but with a specific safety net...
Your call depends on risk tolerance and what your spouse/partner thinks about the financial pressure.
companion_fingerprint_raw.evidence_rationale: Quotes outline balanced alternatives tied to key variables.

companion_fingerprint_raw.reasoning_move: Insist on specific, reality-based alignment from spouse beyond conceptual support to avoid future conflicts.

If your spouse hasn't seen the specific math, their on-board-ness is for a concept, not for the reality.
Supportive-in-concept but not-aligned-on-specifics is exactly how couples end up fighting about money six months into a new business.

companion_fingerprint_raw.reasoning_move: Provide concrete, actionable tactics for de-risking options like fractional roles, including specific asks.

companion_fingerprint_raw.evidence_rationale: Quote delivers precise, executable language for network outreach.
